verses.text: The prince of Tyre's heart is lifted up, saying, 'I am a God, I sit in the seat of God,' though he is but a man. Deadly pride. To set oneself in God's place is the height of folly.

The prince of Tyre's heart is lifted up, saying, 'I am a God, I sit in the seat of God,' though he is but a man. Deadly pride. To set oneself in God's place is the height of folly.
Because he made his heart as God's, strangers shall bring him down to the pit. Pride toppled. The self-exalting are cast down by the very God they usurped.
Because he made his heart as God's, strangers shall bring him down to the pit. Pride toppled. The self-exalting are cast down by the very God they usurped.
Because he made his heart as God's, strangers shall bring him down to the pit. Pride toppled. The self-exalting are cast down by the very God they usurped.
Will he still say 'I am God' before the one who slays him? He shall die 'the deaths of the uncircumcised.' Boasting silenced. Death exposes the emptiness of human pretension.
Will he still say 'I am God' before the one who slays him? He shall die 'the deaths of the uncircumcised.' Boasting silenced. Death exposes the emptiness of human pretension.
A lament for the king of Tyre: 'Thou hast been in Eden the garden of God,' covered with every precious stone. Dazzling height. Behind the earthly king looms the figure of fallen majesty.
A lament for the king of Tyre: 'Thou hast been in Eden the garden of God,' covered with every precious stone. Dazzling height. Behind the earthly king looms the figure of fallen majesty.
A lament for the king of Tyre: 'Thou hast been in Eden the garden of God,' covered with every precious stone. Dazzling height. Behind the earthly king looms the figure of fallen majesty.
He was 'the anointed cherub,' perfect in his ways 'till iniquity was found in thee'; so God casts him out. The fall of pride. Even the most exalted, corrupted by sin, is hurled down.
He was 'the anointed cherub,' perfect in his ways 'till iniquity was found in thee'; so God casts him out. The fall of pride. Even the most exalted, corrupted by sin, is hurled down.
He was 'the anointed cherub,' perfect in his ways 'till iniquity was found in thee'; so God casts him out. The fall of pride. Even the most exalted, corrupted by sin, is hurled down.
His heart was lifted up by his beauty; God casts him to the ground, and he becomes 'a terror... never shalt... be any more.' Beauty made an idol. Self-glory ends in everlasting horror.

Against Zidon: God will be glorified in her judgment, sending pestilence and blood into her streets. His glory in justice. God makes Himself known by judging the proud.

Then Israel shall have no more 'pricking brier'; God will gather them to dwell safely, build, and plant. Peace after judgment. The fall of her enemies secures His people's rest.
